the cost of living in santa fe is much to high. almost everyone has to commute to work here.
we need food at sfcc!
thank you
mucahs gracias por escuchar

column1: Is there anything else you would like to share?
column6: i really appreciate the food pantry on campus and the kindness of the employees overall. many events encourage others to take leftovers.
thanks for the opportunity to provide input by administering this survey.
it was hard to take the survey and admit i have challenges before me.  thank you for conducting this survey.  asking about our well-being was long over due and passed over by other administrations.
multi-point welfare
i hope more funds focus on this

column1: Is there anything else you would like to share?
column6: in one of the text boxes i explained this but i want to share it here so it is obvious. i'm temporary part-time faculty but i'm working over "full-time". i work more than a full-time employee (depending on the semester). this semester i'm 108% employed. despite this insane amount of work, i make very little, worry about saving for retirement, don't have any job security, don't have reliable benefits, and feel my only option to make this work is to live with my parents and eat their food.
it's disgusting that one of the richest countries in the world has students trying to get a good education and going hungry.
offering health insurance to all employees. worrying about receiving any healthcare means going into more debt because bills are beyond affordable and housing and food are more important.
thanks!
safety on campus is a basic need that needs to be addressed
thank you for looking into this issue. it affects all members of out community directly and indirectly, even those like me who aren't currently experiencing food insecurity and housing uncertainty.
help the needy.

each state should provide a totally free on-demand/online option for earning a college degree.  the khan academy, for example, provides subject-matter lessons that are totally free, are available on demand, and are mostly far better at conveying useful information than in-class courses available at practically any of our public universities.
iâ€™d like to see a food library similar to the small libraryâ€™s put on their yard. i would bring food to that to help students and staff.
while in grad school there were insufficient accommodations for mental health, as an adjunct professor this is more than twice as true.
jonen
thank you for asking
thank you for conducting the survey.  i hope it is dovetailing into the strategic planning efforts on campus as a whole and with some of the colleges like sa+p.
inflation is the problem

during the pandemic, several organizations (e.g., afsc) arranged to purchase produce from many small growers in albuquerque for distribution through food banks. could this model be extended to universities to support the consumption of healthy foods by student populations? in addition to paying service providers to run fast food restaurants on campus, could a portion of the budget be used to support local farmers and subsidize the cost of fresh produce on campuses?
this is a multidimensionsl, complex issue. this survey is a great start.
thank you for doing this survey. this affects all of us and itâ€™s all of our responsibility to help find and support solutions
more campus security please
thank you for doing this survey.
thank you for doing this research.

thank you for this outreach to our academic communities. i am happy to be able to contribute to a needs assessment of our school and to have an opportunity to contribute to constructive ideas to improve the quality of life of our students, staff and faculty.

food insecurity is an important issue. i donâ€™t have any insight into how to help address it as an institution. other than to promote the programs and opportunities currently available to students more directly (institution wide emails from deans and chairs!)
thank you for conducting this survey. i hope your findings can help our community members get their basic needs met.
a salad bar somewhere besides la posada (which is intended mainly for students) would be nice.
i believe there is a food waste issue on campus that could be used to help feed those who need.

i feel like a lot of the services i'm being asked about are in person and currently being a remote employee, the service hours being during working hours as well as all the way on campus is frustrating and makes me feel like i'm being excluded in a way.

biology has a food pantry because we found that students are hungry during the day, so we provide a place for them to grab a bite during the day or take home something for supper.  we are aware that hunger is an issue and we are trying to do a little to alleviate that situation for those who need it.
unm should raise salaries for part-time faculty.

i think that unm does a lot to improve the lives of many new mexicans, either through education directly or through good jobs. i'm very happy here. i do think that there are quite a few people who work here (students, graduate students, various faculty positions) whose pay should be increased both for equity and their security.
pay your temporary part time employees wages that align with the current state of inflation.

education is incredibly important for proper nutrition, but for people who donâ€™t have that kind of information it might be a bit more difficult to make those choices. so i think education should be just as much of a part of a pantry as the handing out of food.
everything is connected. one aspect of food, housing, transportation, safety, etc, effects others, they are interconnected and when there is one inequity then there stems many inequities.
the lobo food pantry has helped many of my friends and family in recent years and many other resources that i learned as an undergrad at unm.
i work 2 jobs and go to school
provide more resources for the community
thank you.
a few years ago i was homeless. it was very difficult to find help. i had an advantage that i had a job and an education. many people don't. i can only imagine what others have to go through.
thank you. i hope this survey does actually help someone.
think about staff sick days and how it impacts pay. accrue sick pay at a higher rate to accommodate mental health issues.
advertise more about having resources available. get the community involved too! include athletic events to run food drives, and present those available resources at games.
thank you for researching this important topic!

i rely on abq city busses to get to/from work on unm main campus. reliability of the bus line i take (790) has become increasingly unreliable, especially for the morning route. the bus is often late to pick us up, or won't show up at all.
thank you for doing this survey/study and for trying to help those that do have housing/food insecurities!

the inequity and value difference between faculty and staff is ridiculous and needs to be addressed. staff are resentful and can find better options, and better balance and remote options, outside of unm. the state has a windfall of oil revenue and simply pads their own pockets without addressing wages and crime.
doctors who can afford food are provided meals all day, multiple visits.... employees who make a minimum of 75% less have to pay for their food... doesnt make sense.
thank you for this survey.
la-po made a step in the right direction by giving out to-go boxes for food so students can at least take food home.
i have a partner who makes a good income. if i didn't some of my answers may have been different.
donâ€™t exploit your foreign employees and assume theyâ€™ll take that because theyâ€™re foreigners.
i think that unm does a lot to support the vulnerable students on campus, but more needs to be done.
thank you for doing this survey and for looking for ways to assist those that are in need of food, housing and medical services.
pay staff more, pay regents less
pay your employees more. if you don't get a raise to account for inflation, you are taking a pay cut.
life can be hard, but those times can be great teaching experiences.  make you more determined to succeed.
thank you for conducting the unm basic needs project, including this survey, to create evidence-based solutions.
can't afford health insurance in order to secure food and housing costs
inflation hurts the bottom in the society.

column1: Is there anything else you would like to share?
column6: our students need food options on our campus.
food court on-campus
the cost of living has risen but our salaries haven't kept up.
adjunct professors are under appreciated and underpaid. increase salary is critical.
its nice to see this survey go out, maany people in our area suffer from food and housing insecurity.

when i first started as an adjunct instructor i wasn't allowed to teach more than 12hrs a week, so my take home salary was very low the first year. so low, i had to live with my parents the first year. then when we hired the new instructor, he got full-time employee pay right from the beginning, when i had to struggle my first year working for unm. it was really unfair.

life as an adjunct has its positive moment but most of the leverage i have with the times i works is costing me a lot of income meaning lost income   iâ€™m a poor with an mba and chronic medical conditions that

column1: Is there anything else you would like to share?
column2: University of New Mexico, Los Alamos
column6: raising the pay rates for branch faculty to be the equivalent to alb. faculty is a must: promoting the idea that the value is the same to students, but then devaluing faculty work in this way looks very insincere.
very good

column1: Is there anything else you would like to share?
column6: teach the regents and university leaders about common good

i appreciate that this survey exists. i think the public transportation piece is really important. we were excited about the blue bus, but haven't heard any updates. is that still happening? it would be great if we could have a unm-taos shuttle that goes into town. it would be great advertising for unm-taos and could maybe even increase enrollment.
thank you for gathering this info. hopefully, it will lead to some action to help those in need.
i received a raise in december that helped a lot with any stress i had about making ends meet.
provide support or support groups for domestic violence and substance abuse and or co-dependency.
we need affordable housing in my community and on campus

column1: Is there anything else you would like to share?
column6: thank you for this survey and thank you for caring about nm students
thank you for this survey. i appreciate it!

column1: Is there anything else you would like to share?
column6: zoom events don't work. hr offers all of these stupid online programs that are a waste of energy. don't reward people with shit that isn't good for the planet. stop buying cheap swag that transforms into trash upon receipt. don't shop on amazon.  don't take athletes out for fast food on the road. look at the problem with a wide angle lens instead of putting a band-aid on systematic issues. giving people cheap food is less effective than exploring why they need it. our planet is overpopulated by idiots. stop having babies. the #1 ingredient in baby formula is corn syrup. this is the real problem; you'll never have a chance to get it right with a start like that.
